story.soThat: missing/invalid config ile proje start etmesin
story.tasks:
- Task 1: Create config folder structure (AC: 1) - Subtask 1.1: `src/config/` klasörünü oluştur - Subtask 1.2: Folder structure verify et
- Task 2: Create app.config.ts configuration module (AC: 2) - Subtask 2.1: `src/config/app.config.ts` dosyasını oluştur - Subtask 2.2: ConfigService kullanarak PORT export et (default: 3000) - Subtask 2.3: NODE_ENV export et (development/staging/production) - Subtask 2.4: API_PREFIX export et (default: 'api') - Subtask 2.5: Registered factory pattern ile config export et
- Task 3: Create database.config.ts configuration module (AC: 2) - Subtask 3.1: `src/config/database.config.ts` dosyasını oluştur - Subtask 3.2: DATABASE_URL validation ve export (required, non-empty) - Subtask 3.3: Database provider detection (postgresql vs mongodb from URL) - Subtask 3.4: Connection pooling config export (optional, defaults)
- Task 4: Create jwt.config.ts configuration module (AC: 2) - Subtask 4.1: `src/config/jwt.config.ts` dosyasını oluştur - Subtask 4.2: JWT_SECRET export et (required, min 32 chars) - Subtask 4.3: JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION export et (default: '15m') - Subtask 4.4: JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION export et (default: '7d') - Subtask 4.5: Validation: JWT_SECRET length check (min 32 characters)
- Task 5: Create comprehensive .env.example file (AC: 3) - Subtask 5.1: `.env.example` dosyasını oluştur (root directory) - Subtask 5.2: Application variables ekle (NODE_ENV, PORT, API_PREFIX) - Subtask 5.3: Database variables ekle (DATABASE_URL with examples) - Subtask 5.4: JWT variables ekle (JWT_SECRET, expirations) - Subtask 5.5: Future variables ekle (commented out for Epics 4-7) - Subtask 5.6: Comments ekle: Her variable için açıklama ve örnek değer
- Task 6: Create Joi validation schema (AC: 4) - Subtask 6.1: `src/config/env-validation.schema.ts` dosyasını oluştur - Subtask 6.2: Joi import et ve schema tanımla - Subtask 6.3-6.9: Validation rules for all config variables - Subtask 6.10: Schema export: Joi.object().unknown()
- Task 7: Configure ConfigModule in AppModule (AC: 5) - Subtask 7.1-7.4: ConfigModule.forRoot() configuration - Subtask 7.5: Add ConfigModule to AppModule imports (before PrismaModule) - Subtask 7.6: Test: npm run start:dev ile app başlatma
- Task 8: Implement validation error handling (AC: 6) - Subtask 8.1-8.7: Test invalid config scenarios (missing DATABASE_URL, short JWT_SECRET, invalid NODE_ENV, multiple errors)
- Task 9: Documentation ve integration testing (AC: All) - Subtask 9.1: README.md update et (environment setup section) - Subtask 9.2-9.4: Integration tests (fresh setup, ConfigService access, PrismaService integration)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC-1.7.1: `src/config/` klasörü oluşturulmuş
AC-1.7.2: Config dosyaları oluşturulmuş: - `app.config.ts` (PORT, NODE_ENV, API_PREFIX export ediyor) - `database.config.ts` (DATABASE_URL validation export ediyor) - `jwt.config.ts` (JWT_SECRET, JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION, JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION export ediyor)
AC-1.7.3: `.env.example` oluşturulmuş (tüm required variables placeholder ile)
AC-1.7.4: Config validation Joi schema ile (ConfigModule.forRoot içinde validationSchema)
AC-1.7.5: AppModule'de ConfigModule.forRoot() configured (isGlobal: true, validationSchema: envSchema)
AC-1.7.6: Invalid config ile start attempt descriptive error veriyor (Joi validation error message console'da, process exit)

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Database Infrastructure & Project Setup
doc.snippet: AC-1.7.1 through AC-1.7.6 define complete configuration system setup with Joi validation schema. ConfigModule.forRoot() must be configured with isGlobal:true, validationSchema, and multi-env file support (.env.development, .env). Fail-fast approach: application won't start with invalid config.

doc.title: Boilerplate - Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Developer wants environment-based configuration and validation so that project won't start with missing/invalid config. Config files: app.config.ts (PORT, NODE_ENV, API_PREFIX), database.config.ts (DATABASE_URL), jwt.config.ts (JWT secrets and expirations). Joi validation with fail-fast approach.

doc.title: Architecture - Boilerplate
doc.snippet: Required environment variables: NODE_ENV, PORT, API_PREFIX, DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET (min 32 chars), JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRATION (default: 15m), JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION (default: 7d). ConfigModule.forRoot() with Joi validation schema. Environment file priority: .env.development > .env. Fail-fast validation on application startup.

doc.title: Architecture - Boilerplate
doc.snippet: Joi validation schema pattern: string().min(32).required() for JWT_SECRET, valid('development','staging','production').required() for NODE_ENV, number().default(3000) for PORT. validationOptions: { abortEarly: false, allowUnknown: true } to show all errors and allow future epic variables.

doc.title: Story 1.6: Seed Data Script - Dev Notes
doc.snippet: DATABASE_URL already in use from Story 1.3 setup and Story 1.4 PrismaService. Story 1.7 will ADD validation layer on top of existing DATABASE_URL usage. PrismaService connects using process.env.DATABASE_URL. This story formalizes config management and adds fail-fast validation. Epic 1 final story - completes infrastructure foundation.

artifact.reason: Current AppModule imports PrismaModule only. Will be updated to import ConfigModule.forRoot() BEFORE PrismaModule to ensure config is validated before database connection.

Existing .env.example has basic structure. Will be updated/replaced with comprehensive template including all required variables, proper comments, and placeholders for future epics (AWS S3, Twilio, SendGrid, Sentry).
Current runtime env has DATABASE_URL, NODE_ENV, PORT. Missing JWT_SECRET and API_PREFIX. After story completion, these will be required by Joi validation schema.

PrismaService currently uses process.env.DATABASE_URL directly. After story completion, PrismaService should inject ConfigService to access validated DATABASE_URL from ConfigService.get('DATABASE_URL').

Current dependencies include @nestjs/common, @nestjs/core, @nestjs/platform-express. Missing: @nestjs/config (for ConfigModule) and joi (for validation schema). These need to be added as dependencies.

package.name: @nestjs/config
package.reason: Official NestJS configuration module for environment variable management and validation

package.name: joi
package.reason: Schema validation library for environment variable validation (supports min/max, type checking, required fields, custom rules)

package.name: @prisma/client
package.usage: Prisma client for database access (uses validated DATABASE_URL)

story-context.constraints:
1. **Architecture Constraint**: ConfigModule MUST be imported BEFORE PrismaModule in AppModule to ensure configuration is validated before database connection attempts. [Source: docs/architecture.md#Project-Initialization]
2. **Security Constraint**: JWT_SECRET MUST be minimum 32 characters. Joi validation schema must enforce this with `.min(32)`. [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-1.md#NFR-Security]
3. **Validation Constraint**: Joi validation options MUST include `abortEarly: false` to show ALL validation errors at once, not just the first error. This improves developer experience. [Source: docs/architecture.md#Configuration-Validation]
4. **Flexibility Constraint**: Joi schema MUST include `.unknown()` to allow extra environment variables not explicitly defined. This supports future epic variables (AWS S3, Twilio, SendGrid, Sentry) without breaking validation. [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-1.md#AC-1.7.4]
5. **File Structure Constraint**: Config files MUST use kebab-case naming (app.config.ts, database.config.ts, jwt.config.ts) and be placed in `src/config/` directory. [Source: docs/architecture.md#Project-Structure, ADR-008]
6. **Environment File Priority**: ConfigModule.forRoot() MUST specify envFilePath as array: ['.env.development', '.env'] to support environment-specific config files with fallback. [Source: docs/architecture.md#Environment-Configuration]
7. **Fail-Fast Approach**: Application MUST exit with descriptive error message if any required config variable is missing or invalid. Joi validation handles this automatically when validationSchema is provided. [Source: docs/architecture.md#Configuration-Validation]
8. **Integration Constraint**: PrismaService should be updated to use ConfigService for DATABASE_URL instead of direct process.env access. This ensures config validation happened before database connection. However, direct process.env fallback is acceptable for Epic 1 MVP. [Source: Story 1.4 integration notes]
9. **Testing Constraint**: Task 8 requires testing multiple invalid config scenarios: missing DATABASE_URL, short JWT_SECRET (< 32 chars), invalid NODE_ENV value, and multiple simultaneous errors to verify abortEarly:false works correctly.
10. **Documentation Constraint**: README.md MUST be updated with environment setup instructions including .env.example → .env copy process and required variables list. [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-1.md#AC-1.7.3]

interface.name: ConfigModule.forRoot()
ConfigService.get<T>()
interface.signature: get<T = string>(key: string, defaultValue?: T): T | undefined // Examples: configService.get<number>('PORT') configService.get<string>('DATABASE_URL') configService.get<string>('JWT_SECRET')

interface.name: Joi Validation Schema
Environment Variables

tests.standards:
NestJS projects use Jest as testing framework (configured in package.json).
For configuration modules, focus on integration tests that verify: 1. Application boots successfully with valid config 2. Application fails to boot with missing required variables (DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET) 3. Application fails to boot with invalid values (JWT_SECRET < 32 chars, NODE_ENV not in valid list) 4. Joi validation shows all errors when multiple config issues present (abortEarly: false) 5. Default values are applied correctly (PORT=3000, API_PREFIX='api') 6. ConfigService.get() returns correct values from environment
Unit tests are not typically needed for config modules since they primarily declare configuration. Focus on integration testing the ConfigModule setup in AppModule and validation behavior.
tests.locations:
Integration tests (recommended for config validation): - test/app.e2e-spec.ts (E2E test for application bootstrap with various config scenarios)
Unit tests (optional, low priority): - src/config/__tests__/env-validation.schema.spec.ts (if complex custom validators added)
Config validation is best tested by attempting to start the application with different .env configurations and verifying startup success/failure.
tests.ideas:
Test Idea 1 (AC-1.7.6): Test invalid DATABASE_URL missing - Create temporary .env with DATABASE_URL commented out - Attempt to start application (npm run start:dev or NestFactory.create) - Verify application exits with error message: "DATABASE_URL" is required - Verify process exit code is non-zero
Test Idea 2 (AC-1.7.6): Test invalid JWT_SECRET (too short) - Create temporary .env with JWT_SECRET="short" (less than 32 chars) - Attempt to start application - Verify error message: "JWT_SECRET" length must be at least 32 characters long - Verify application does not start
Test Idea 3 (AC-1.7.6): Test invalid NODE_ENV value - Create temporary .env with NODE_ENV="invalid_environment" - Attempt to start application - Verify error message: "NODE_ENV" must be one of [development, staging, production] - Verify application does not start
Test Idea 4 (AC-1.7.6): Test multiple validation errors (abortEarly: false) - Create temporary .env with DATABASE_URL missing AND JWT_SECRET="short" - Attempt to start application - Verify BOTH error messages are displayed (not just the first one) - This verifies abortEarly: false is working correctly
Test Idea 5 (AC-1.7.5): Test successful startup with valid config - Create .env with all required variables and valid values - Start application successfully - Verify ConfigService.get('PORT') returns 3000 (or custom value) - Verify ConfigService.get('DATABASE_URL') returns correct connection string - Verify ConfigService.get('JWT_SECRET') returns secret (min 32 chars)
Test Idea 6 (AC-1.7.4): Test default values applied - Create .env with only required variables (DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, NODE_ENV) - Omit PORT and API_PREFIX - Start application - Verify ConfigService.get('PORT') returns 3000 (default) - Verify ConfigService.get('API_PREFIX') returns 'api' (default)
Test Idea 7 (Integration): Test PrismaService uses validated config - After Story 1.7 completion, verify PrismaService connects to database - Database connection should only be attempted AFTER config validation passes - If DATABASE_URL is invalid, validation should fail before Prisma connection attempt - This verifies ConfigModule is imported BEFORE PrismaModule in AppModule
